Task.title: Task
Task.description: Represents a task within the TaskFlow application.

description.description: Detailed description of the task, supports rich text formatting.

priority.description: Priority level of the task (P0, P1, P2, P3).

status.description: Current status of the task (Yet to Start, WIP, In Review, Done).

StatusHistory.title: StatusHistory
StatusHistory.description: Represents the history of status changes for a task.

id.description: Unique identifier for the status history entry.

taskId.description: Reference to the Task entity. (Relationship: Task 1:N StatusHistory)

status.description: The status that the task was changed to.

timestamp.description: Timestamp when the status was changed.

definition.description: Stores task information for a specific user. Path-based ownership is enforced through the `userId` parameter.

params.name: userId
params.description: The ID of the user who owns the task.

definition.description: Stores the status history for a particular task. The nested path enforces ownership via `userId` and `taskId`.

params.name: userId
params.description: The ID of the user who owns the task.

params.name: taskId
params.description: The ID of the parent task.

params.name: statusHistoryId
params.description: The ID of the status history entry.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to provide a secure and scalable solution for the TaskFlow application, adhering to the principles of Authorization Independence, Clarity of Intent, DBAC, and QAPs.

**Authorization Independence:** Tasks and their status histories are owned by individual users. The structure leverages path-based ownership (`/users/{userId}/tasks/{taskId}`) to enforce security. No `get()` calls are required in the security rules as authorization context is derived directly from the path. The `userId` is implicitly the owner, providing Authorization Independence.

**Structural Segregation:** Tasks are segregated by user ID, ensuring that each user's tasks have the same security posture.

**Access Modeling:** Path-based ownership is used for private data (tasks owned by a specific user).

**QAPs:** List operations are secured by scoping tasks to the user's ID. A user can only list tasks under their own `/users/{userId}/tasks` path, making rules simple and secure.

The structure facilitates easy debugging due to its clear and explicit path-based ownership and avoids complex rule logic by denormalizing ownership through path structure.
